title: delphinium scopulorum subsp. occidentale
intro: Delphinium scopulorum subsp. occidentale, also known as Western larkspur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the delphinium genus, characterized by its tall spikes of blue or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Western larkspur include its potential as a treatment for various ailments due to its high concentration of delphinine and other alkaloids. The health benefits of Western larkspur are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as muscle spasms, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Western larkspur is typically prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea for medicinal purposes, but caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
